question.text: The Association of Investment Bankers of India (AIBI) was originally founded in 1993 under which name?

answer.text: Association of Capital Market Intermediaries of India
Association of Merchant Bankers of India
Association of Securities Professionals of India
Association of Issue Managers of India

explanation.text: **The Logic:** AIBI was originally founded in 1993 as the "Association of Merchant Bankers of India" and was later renamed. It was granted recognition by SEBI as a self-regulatory organization and serves as the industry's sole representative to statutory authorities. **The Trap:** The other options sound plausible as names for a financial industry body, but only "Association of Merchant Bankers of India" is the historically correct original name. **Cross-Exam Context:** Self-regulatory organizations (SROs) and their roles are discussed in NISM Series IX and are relevant to understanding market governance. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** AIBI plays a dual role — it is both a self-regulatory organization recognized by SEBI and a thought leader for the merchant banking industry. It serves as the sole industry voice to SEBI.
